prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='DWT_rf', seasonality_champion='ETS', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.788) and high seasonality variance (1.0907), with early horizon specialists (DWT_rf, THETA) differing from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost). The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds (up, down, down), requiring models that can adapt to changing trends. DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating disagreement risks.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "DWT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "ETS",
  "overall_champion": "THETA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "DWT_rf", "late": "DWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["THETA", "ETS", "DWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_FT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_rf", "FT_catboost", "ARIMA", "CWT_catboost", "DWT_svr", "DWT_catboost", "FT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.788,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.788) and high seasonality variance (1.0907), with early horizon specialists (DWT_rf, THETA) differing from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost). The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds (up, down, down), requiring models that can adapt to changing trends. DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating disagreement risks."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "robust_median"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.35}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=5 indicates horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection. relative_spread_mean=0.275 (≥0.25) and rmse_spread_ratio=0.788 favor robust methods like dba_combination and robust_median. Pattern analyst explicitly recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (1.0907). topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 with shrinkage=0.35 reduces instability from n_windows=3. inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 leverages performance differences between THETA (RMSE=2440.9) and worst models."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has good diversity: 2 baselines (dba_combination, robust_median), 2 selections (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). All candidates are valid from current_candidate_names. Universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation (score 0.754) and ridge_stacking variants (score ~0.807) but current set scores are higher (0.815-0.840). No leakage issues - inverse_rmse_weights uses anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection. No redundancy requiring pruning. Validation has n_windows=3, horizon=12, which is sufficient for weighted candidates.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.788) and high seasonality variance (1.0907), with early horizon specialists (DWT_rf, THETA) differing from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost). The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds (up, down, down), requiring models that can adapt to changing trends. DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating disagreement risks.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=5 indicates horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection. relative_spread_mean=0.275 (≥0.25) and rmse_spread_ratio=0.788 favor robust methods like dba_combination and robust_median. Pattern analyst explicitly recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (1.0907). topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 with shrinkage=0.35 reduces instability from n_windows=3. inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 leverages performance differences between THETA (RMSE=2440.9) and worst models.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has good diversity: 2 baselines (dba_combination, robust_median), 2 selections (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). All candidates are valid from current_candidate_names. Universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation (score 0.754) and ridge_stacking variants (score ~0.807) but current set scores are higher (0.815-0.840). No leakage issues - inverse_rmse_weights uses anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection. No redundancy requiring pruning. Validation has n_windows=3, horizon=12, which is sufficient for weighted candidates.
